title: What Are Embeddings?
subtitle: RAG & Vector Search — Like One Academy

sections.text: What if every word had an address?
sections.subtext: A location in mathematical space that captures its meaning

sections.text: An embedding is a list of numbers that represents the meaning of text. Think of it as giving every word a unique address in mathematical space. Two words that mean similar things get addresses that are close together. Happy lives next to joyful. Sad lives far away from both.

sections.label: Embedding Space
sections.subtitle: A map where meaning has coordinates

sections.text: Here is why this matters. When you ask an AI a question, it does not search for matching words. It searches for matching meaning. Your question becomes a point in space, and the AI finds the closest answers — even if they use completely different words. That is semantic search. That is the magic.

The numbers themselves are not random. Each dimension captures something about meaning. One dimension might track sentiment. Another might track formality. Another might capture topic. A seven-hundred-dimensional embedding captures seven hundred different aspects of what a word means. That is richer than any dictionary.

sections.label: in a typical embedding — each one captures an aspect of meaning

sections.text: This is how your AI brain works. Every memory gets embedded. Every question gets embedded. And the closest memories become context for the answer. Embeddings are the foundation of RAG, of semantic search, of everything that makes AI feel like it understands you.

sections.heading: Next: How Vector Search Works
sections.subtext: From embeddings to finding needles in haystacks.
